family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
strongest_honest_claim: The zero-divisor campaign now has paired verified p = 13 feeders on both active lines: Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) survives with a refined small-spill C block, and Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) survives the first zero-slack six-class test with the unrefined C = 1 plus {2,3}-smooth template. The Lean surface now also contains checked abstract support assets `support_decomposition_F25`, `support_decomposition_F2`, `classwise_template_lemma`, `zp_z25_support_template_reduction`, and `zp_zp_z2_support_template_reduction_of_singleton_one`. The main remaining gap is no longer whether the paired p = 13 frontier survives, but whether the actual ring-to-support bridge lemmas can be formalized cleanly enough to turn this feeder cluster into a paper-ready family theorem slice.
paper_title_hint: Support-template reductions and paired p = 13 feeders in two zero-divisor prime-labeling families
theorem_slice_target: Family-level bridge lemmas from the ring law into the checked support-template reductions for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2), together with the paired verified p = 13 arithmetic checkpoints.
primary_theorem_slice_statement: For odd prime p, prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise coprimality allocation problems on tiny support graphs. On the four-class family, the honest arithmetic statement uses a small-spill C block whose exceptional below-half primes send their nontrivial multiples to D. On the six-class family, the actual support graph is A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D, so after setting the singleton class C to label 1 only A-B, A-F, and B-E remain parameter-sensitive.
fallback_theorem_slice_statement: If the family bridge lemmas or later arithmetic extension still stall, preserve the paired p = 13 slice exactly as it now stands: Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) proves the old upper-half-prime-only C rule is too rigid even though the graph remains prime after refinement, and Γ(Z_13 × Z_13 × Z_2) proves the naive {2,3}-smooth six-class template survives exactly at the first zero-slack boundary.
missing_support_decomposition_lemmas: f25_ring_support_partition_lemma: classify every nonzero zero-divisor of Z_p × Z_25 into the four support classes A, B, C, D.
f25_ring_support_adjacency_lemma: prove from the ring law that zero product occurs exactly on the class pairs A-C, B-B, B-C, and B-D.
f25_family_wrapper_bridge_lemma: instantiate the abstract F25 support-template theorem from the actual ring-level partition and adjacency facts.
f2_ring_support_partition_lemma: classify every nonzero zero-divisor of Z_p × Z_p × Z_2 into the six support classes A, B, C, D, E, F.
f2_ring_support_adjacency_lemma: prove from the ring law that zero product occurs exactly on the class pairs A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, and C-D.
f2_family_wrapper_bridge_lemma: instantiate the abstract F2 support-template theorem from the actual ring-level partition and adjacency facts.
parameter_sensitive_steps: Supply p - 1 C-labels for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) while allowing a bounded number of below-half primes whose nontrivial multiples are pushed into D and still keeping A ∪ B clear of every C-prime factor.
Choose a four-label sparse barrier set for the B clique in Γ(Z_p × Z_25) whose nontrivial multiples fit inside the 20 available A slots.
Count enough labels for A ∪ E in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) supported on a fixed small prime set S; the verified p = 13 feeder now shows the naive S = {2,3} program survives exactly at zero slack.
Pack B ∪ F as a disjoint complement block avoiding every prime in S while leaving the D block unconstrained after the singleton-one reduction.
fallback_target: If the arithmetic corollaries do not yet close, preserve the structural slice plus the paired p = 13 arithmetic statement rather than overclaiming a full odd-prime theorem.
next_blocker: The campaign still needs the actual ring-to-support bridge lemmas that connect the new Lean support module to the family rings themselves, and it still needs one post-p = 13 stress test on the refined four-class line to see how much p-range language is honest.
next_feeder_why_decisive: The paired p = 13 frontier is now cleared on both active lines, so the next arithmetic discriminator is the smallest post-13 test on the four-class family. Γ(Z_17 × Z_25) is decisive because it asks whether the refined small-spill C-block strategy is a genuine family corollary or only a local repair at p = 13.
